name: Overprovisioned default Compose stack
summary: Starting every service by default in local or default compose profiles wastes local compute and encourages unnecessary background activity.
description: Compose files that always start optional databases, observability services, caches, and workers even when they are not needed consume developer resources and energy for little value.

impact.performance: Consumes memory and CPU that could be used elsewhere.
impact.carbon: Runs more services than needed for the task at hand.

remediation.guidance: Use profiles or separate compose overlays so services start only when needed.
examples: Make observability and auxiliary services opt-in rather than default.
